title: rubus horriduliformis
intro: Rubus horriduliformis, also known as the thorny blackberry, is a type of bramble native to Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Rubus horriduliformis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves, stems, and roots can be used in herbal preparations, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
